description: ConocoPhillips is a Houston-based independent exploration and production company focused on crude oil, natural gas, NGLs, bitumen, and LNG-linked assets across the United States and selected international basins.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: ConocoPhillips is one of the world's largest independent upstream oil and gas companies, with operations organized around exploration, development, production, transportation, and marketing rather than refining or retail fuels. Its portfolio spans Lower 48 unconventional basins, Alaska, Canada, Europe, the Middle East, North Africa, and Asia Pacific.

The company is best understood as a scale E&P operator: it turns acreage, drilling programs, joint ventures, and commodity exposure into cash flow, then explains the volatility with the calm vocabulary of capital discipline.

overview.title: Core Operations
paragraphs: The Lower 48 segment is the company's largest operating engine, anchored by unconventional oil and gas positions in the Permian, Eagle Ford, Bakken, and related U.S. resource plays. Recent annual disclosures identify Lower 48 production as the majority contributor to consolidated liquids and natural gas output.

Outside the contiguous United States, ConocoPhillips maintains material positions in Alaska, Canadian oil sands and gas assets, Norway, Libya, Malaysia, Australia, and Qatar-linked LNG ventures. The result is a portfolio that is geographically diversified, though still firmly tied to global hydrocarbon demand.

overview.title: LNG And Gas Strategy
paragraphs: ConocoPhillips has a long-running LNG presence through projects and technology licensing, including interests connected to QatarEnergy LNG and other international gas value chains. LNG gives the company exposure to global gas demand and long-duration infrastructure contracts, which can look more elegant on investor slides than on climate transition scorecards.

The LNG portfolio should be treated as a distinct product and infrastructure theme in the registry because it combines upstream gas resources, liquefaction partnerships, marketing capacity, and proprietary liquefaction technology.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: For Free The World, ConocoPhillips belongs in the energy registry as a major incumbent in fossil fuel extraction whose products sit upstream of transportation, power, petrochemicals, industrial heat, and LNG trade. The most relevant comparison set is not consumer software or devices, but open alternatives in energy data transparency, grid flexibility, electrification, public-interest climate tooling, and decentralized energy systems.

Initial product mapping should separate operating regions from market-facing energy products: Lower 48 operations, Alaska operations, LNG portfolio, oil sands exposure, and global exploration/production assets. That structure will make later alternative mapping less likely to collapse into the usual corporate soup of barrels, basins, and branding.

sourceSuggestions.title: ConocoPhillips Alaska
